title: Surface blast-radius invariants by default, not opt-in
context: Hunch checks invariants to prevent breaking constraints when editing. Previously, only direct invariants (in the file's scope) were checked—invariants you could break *indirectly* via dependent files required explicit blast-radius queries.
decision: Make blast-radius invariants ('near-violations') a default, always-on concern across all surfaces: CLI pre-commit guard now flags direct OR near invariants by default; new MCP tool `hunch_blast_radius` surfaces them to Claude before editing; VS Code extension displays them in status bar and brief. Opt-out via `--no-enforce`, not opt-in.
consequences: Developers see indirect risks without extra steps—shifts mental model from 'check this file' to 'check this file + what depends on it'
Pre-commit guard scope expands: catches near-violations in advisory mode (always flags), strict mode (blocks on blocking near-violations)
VS Code extension UI grows: status bar shows '+N near' count, why-brief adds near-invariants section, bug tree shows lineage markers
alternatives_rejected: Keep blast-radius as advanced opt-in feature (requires deliberate action, hides risks by default)
Check only direct invariants in default view (exposes developers to silent indirect breakage)
Add MCP tool but don't integrate into CLI/extension default behavior (tool exists but isn't used)
